_comment:
Six-position accelerometer calibration. Operator orients the airframe through each face (level, on side, on other side, nose up, nose down, on back) while the autopilot collects samples and computes per-axis bias + scale.
Shape mirrors compass cal: single step, success-or-escalate, with operator-action escalations on the two well-known autopilot refusal modes.
- calibrationDeclined → autopilot wouldn't even start (no stable level reference, vehicle moving, etc.) → ask operator to put it on a level surface.
- calibrationDidNotConverge → procedure started but samples didn't converge (operator was too fast, missed a face, vehicle moved during sampling) → ask operator to repeat the orientation pass.
Overall budget is 300s — the manual six-position dance typically takes 2-4 minutes; the cap is generous but well under the 600s parser ceiling.
Auto-retries (timeout / noSession / autopilotBusy) are handled by the catalogue's default retry policy on the dispatch itself, so the matchers below only see the *final* outcome.

then.detail: Accelerometer calibration failed with an unhandled outcome — see the audit trace for the underlying response.
